channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Apple will reportedly allow other AI chatbots to plug into Siri
item.description: Apple's iOS 27 update will allow users to choose the AI chatbot they want to link with Siri. That's according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, who says third-party chatbots downloaded from the App Store, like Google's Gemini or Anthropic's Claude, will be able to fetch replies for Siri - similar to how the […]

item.title: X moves the ashes of Tweetdeck behind its $40 Premium+ subscription
item.description: X Pro, the feature most users would recognize as TweetDeck, has been removed as a benefit of the social network's Premium subscription. It is now only part of the Premium+ tier, which costs $40 a month. TweetDeck was rebranded to X Pro in 2023 following Elon Musk's renaming of Twitter to X. It became a subscription feature shortly after. The tool offered a popular interface for showing multiple timelines, feeds and lists in a single interface.Engadget staffers using X Pro at the Premium level didn't find any advanced notice that the feature would be changing subscription tiers, so people may b...

item.title: Trans women athletes are banned from the Olympic Games starting in 2028
item.description: Eligibility will be determined by gene screening via saliva, cheek swab, or blood sample. Transgender women athletes are now excluded from all women’s events at the Olympic Games in accordance with a new policy from the International Olympic Committee (IOC).

item.title: Why ‘For All Mankind’ Season 5 Is the Key to the Rest of the Series
item.description: With season six now official, io9 spoke to the 'For All Mankind' showrunners about the pivotal new season.

item.title: Netflix augmente encore ses prix aux États-Unis, l’Europe pourrait bientôt passer à la caisse
item.description: Arrêtez-nous si vous avez déjà entendu celle-là : Netflix a encore augmenté ses prix. Pas en France heureusement, mais aux États-Unis. C'est la deuxième augmentation en un peu plus d'un an. Et on ne peut que se préparer psychologiquement à l'annonce d'une hausse en Europe.

item.title: Anker’s wireless charging pad offers Qi2 speeds for $15
item.description: I’ve probably saved minutes of my life by using a wireless charging puck. Minutes! More valuable than that, I’ve saved myself from bending over numerous times to pick up my USB-C cable that somehow always ends up on the floor. Those with magnet-equipped phones (like the iPhone 12 or newer, or a Google Pixel 10 […]

item.title: New Netflix series Something Very Bad is Going to Happen isn't actually an ode to The Blair Witch Project — and these sneaky horror movie Easter eggs prove it
item.description: Something Very Bad is Going to Happen makes an obvious reference to The Blair Witch Project — but the show's hidden Easter eggs are an ode to another classic horror movie.

item.title: Apple Music’s Concert page is the update I’ve been waiting for — but it has a big flaw that makes it more frustrating than it should be
item.description: Apple Music Concerts page is here, and the feature is almost perfect for live music lovers.

item.title: You’re suddenly told to WFH tomorrow. Are you about to get fired over Zoom?
item.description: Some Meta employees were reportedly asked to work from home on Wednesday—right before the company announced layoffs. Meta laid off hundreds of employees this week, just months after notable cuts to its virtual reality and metaverse division. These job losses amount to less than one percent of the company’s overall workforce, reportedly impacting about 700 employees across a number of departments. But recent headlines indicate there’s likely more to come: Earlier this month, Reuters reported that Meta was planning large-scale cuts to its workforce that could slash 20% of jobs—or more—to help o...

item.title: Apple’s AI Playlist Playground is bad at music
item.description: Apple Music: "What do you want to hear?" Me: "Atmospheric instrumental black metal to write to." Apple Music: "Here's three metal songs with vocals, a field recording, an ambient electronic track, and a piece of doom jazz." I am skeptical of AI's ability to serve up the music I want to begin with, but even […]

item.title: Our favorite web hosting company is providing access to AI's latest superstar for free: One click gets you OpenClaw on Hostinger's shared hosting
item.description: Hostinger enables instant OpenClaw deployment with integrated AI credits and managed infrastructure, simplifying how users run always-on AI agents.

item.title: Mastodon is making its decentralized social network easier to use with its latest revamp
item.description: Mastodon is redesigning user profiles to simplify the experience and better appeal to mainstream users and organizations.

item.title: Netflix confirms it’s raising prices again
item.description: The standard plan without ads now costs $19.99 per month, a $2 increase from the previous $17.99 subscription fee, while the premium plan is also going up by $2 and will now cost $26.99 per month.

item.title: Early Wildfires Are Surging Across the US—and Trump’s New Fire Agency Isn’t Ready
item.description: A brutal wildfire season is about to put Trump’s Wildland Fire Service to the test, with the safety of millions of Americans on the line.

item.title: The ‘PlayStation Car’ is dead — why Sony and Honda just pulled the plug on their Afeela EV before a single car was delivered
item.description: Sony Honda Mobility shelves the much-hyped Afeela 1 EV project amid turbulent times, but the car was ill-fated from the beginning

item.title: Ugh, Netflix is raising prices again
item.description: Netflix is raising prices across all of its subscription tiers, according to an updated "Plans and Pricing" page spotted by Android Authority. The company last raised prices in January 2025, when the cost of all of its tiers were jacked up by $1 or more. As of this latest price hike, Netflix's ad-supported Standard plan is going from $8 per month to $9 per month, while the ad-free version is rising from $18 to $20 per month. The company's Premium plan, meanwhile, which supports things like 4K streams, spatial audio and the ability to watch content on four devices at the same time, is jumping f...

item.title: Attorney Hit With Historic Fine for Citing AI-Generated Cases
item.description: Don't bother asking AI if the cases are real.

item.title: Saturne comme vous ne l’avez presque jamais vue : Hubble et James-Webb révèlent des détails saisissants
item.description: Saturne est observée par des télescopes et des lunettes astronomiques depuis des siècles, mais on aurait tort de croire que tous ses secrets ont été percés, même en utilisant des sondes spatiales. La Nasa et l'ESA poursuivent donc leurs observations, au moins à des fins de surveillance, comme le...

item.title: Travelers Experiencing the Longest Security Lines in TSA History
item.description: Some airports are seeing 4-hour wait times, according to TSA Deputy Administrator Ha Nguyen McNeill.

item.title: Could There Be a ‘Project Hail Mary’ Sequel?
item.description: A new report says it all depends on author Andy Weir, who is considering a follow-up to his best-seller turned blockbuster movie.

item.title: Meta Has More Smart Glasses Coming Whether You Want Them or Not
item.description: Privacy be damned.

item.title: How to watch iHeartRadio Music Awards 2026 for free online from anywhere
item.description: With Taylor Swift, Alex Warren, Bad Bunny and Sabrina Carpenter looking set to win big, here's how to watch iHeartRadio Music Awards 2026 from anywhere.

item.title: ‘The fastest mobile platform for web browsing’: Google claims Android is up to 47% faster than iOS in this key metric
item.description: Google says its Android operating system provides up to 47% faster web browsing speeds than Apple’s iOS.

item.title: Bad news: Your pets may be ruining your sleep
item.description: Research shows that sleeping with your dog or cat in bed brings emotional comfort—but it could cost you a good night’s rest. Many people enjoy sleeping with their pets. Who wouldn’t? After a hard day of work, cuddling up with a cute animal that shows you unconditional love is just the thing many people need.

item.title: How to watch England vs. Uruguay online for free
item.description: How to watch live football for free. Live stream England vs. Uruguay in a 2026 International Friendly for free.

item.title: I test headphones and earbuds nearly every day. These are the best deals on both during Amazons Big Spring Sale.
item.description: I spotted over 35 worthwhile headphones and earbuds deals live during Amazon's Spring Sale, from the newly announced Apple AirPods Max 2 to my favorite pair of sleep earbuds.

item.title: I test laptops for a living. Here are my favorite MacBook deals from Amazons Big Spring Sale.
item.description: We found the best MacBook deals at Amazon during the Big Spring Sale. Get a MacBook Neo for $689 and M4 MacBook Airs for $949, saving $250.

item.title: TSA lines are chaos—and this $209 airport hack is exploding right now
item.description: As a DHS shutdown snarls security nationwide, Clear sign-ups are surging—with app downloads up 674% in a single day. A clear winner is emerging from the chaos at airports this month: Clear Secure.
